Q8 MCQ Not checked

Which of the following statements is/are true?
S1 : BFS take Ο(V + E) time in a graph G(V, E) if graph is represent with an adjacent list.
S2 : The minium spanning tree can be found using BFS if the graph has equal non-negative edge weights.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
S1 : Time complexity of BFS using adjacent list Ο(V + E).
S2 : The minium spanning tree can be found using BFS if the graph has equal non-negative edge weights.

Correct answer: B, C, D
Solution

(b, c, d)
Level order traversal technique is based on BFS.
Preorder, postorder and inorder tree traversal technique is based on DFS.

Q23 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following is/are correct regarding AVL tree?
S1 : In an AVL tree, during the insert operation there are atmost two rotations required.
S2 : AVL tree can be used to implement an optimal comparison based sorting algorithm.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
S1 : The AVL property is restored on every operation. Therefore inserting another item will require at most two rotations to restore the balance.
S2 : AVL tree can be used to sort N numbers in Ο(N log N) time, by inserting all the numbers in the tree, and iteratively calling next largest N times.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
DFS is well-suited for cycle detection because it can efficiently find back edges indicating cycle,making. It the most efficient for the purpose.

Q25 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: B, A, C, D
Solution

(b)
(a) False: A min-heap can provide next largest element, we need to do a linear search through the heap’s array it will take Ο(n) time.
(b) True: Finding the next smallest element, the predecessor, may require traveling down the height of the tree, making the running time Ο(n).
(c) False: Binary insertion sorting improve the time it take to find right position for next element being inserted. It may still take Ο(n) time to perform swaps necessary to shift it into place.
This results is an Ο(n2) running time, the same as that of insertion sort.
(d) False: The worst case unbalanced tree is a list, and balancing it require Ο(n) rotations.

Which of the following is/are true?
S1 : Given a weighted directed graph G = (V, E) containing no zero or positive-weight cycles, Bellman-Ford algorithm can find longest (maximum weight) path vertex s to vertex t.
S2 : Given a weighted directed graph G = (V, E) containing a negative weight cycle, running the
Bellman-Ford algorithm from S will find a shortest acyclic path from s to a given destination vertex t.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
